identity.name: Learning Path
identity.definition: A Learning Path is a personalized, adaptive sequence of instructional activities and practice resources generated from assessment data and aligned to specific skills.

representations.heading: Card layout
representations.description: How a Learning Path appears in student dashboards and teacher views

attributes.name: Path Name
attributes.description: Auto-generated name based on subject and focus skills.

attributes.name: Student
attributes.description: The student this path belongs to.

attributes.name: Status
attributes.description: Current state: active, complete, paused, or stale.

representations.heading: List layout
representations.description: How a Learning Path appears in teacher class views

attributes.name: Student
attributes.description: The student this path belongs to.

representations.heading: Detail layout
representations.description: Full Learning Path view with activity sequence

attributes.name: Student
attributes.description: The student this path belongs to.

attributes.name: Current Level
attributes.description: Student's current proficiency level in the path's subject.

attributes.name: Target Level
attributes.description: Target proficiency level the path aims to reach.

attributes.name: Activities
attributes.description: Ordered sequence of activities in this path.

attributes.name: Generated From
attributes.description: The assessment whose results triggered path generation.

attributes.name: Last Updated
attributes.description: When the path was last adapted based on new data.

attributes.name: Estimated Remaining
attributes.description: Estimated time to complete the remaining activities.

stories.title: View Student's Learning Path
stories.benefit: I can see the personalized sequence of activities the system has generated and understand how the student is progressing toward their skill targets.
stories.whenClause: I navigate to a student's Learning Path,
stories.thenClause: I see the path name, focus skills, current vs. target levels, progress bar, and the ordered list of upcoming activities with their types and estimated times.

stories.title: Continue Learning Path Activity
stories.benefit: I can continue my personalized learning sequence and make progress toward my skill goals.
stories.whenClause: I open my Learning Path and click Start Next Activity,
stories.thenClause: the next activity in my sequence launches, and when I complete it, my path progress updates and the system may adapt upcoming activities based on my performance.

businessRules.title: System Generation
businessRules.description: Learning Paths are generated by the system after a student completes a qualifying assessment (Star Reading or Star Math). Teachers cannot create paths from scratch.

businessRules.title: One Path Per Subject
businessRules.description: A student has at most one active Learning Path per subject (ELA and Math). A new path replaces the previous one for that subject.

businessRules.title: Adaptive Sequencing
businessRules.description: The activity sequence adapts based on student performance. When a student completes an activity, the system may reorder or replace upcoming activities based on demonstrated proficiency.

businessRules.title: Teacher Override
businessRules.description: Teachers can customize a path by accepting/rejecting recommended activities, adding custom assignments, or reordering the sequence. Customizations persist through subsequent system updates.

businessRules.title: Staleness Detection
businessRules.description: A path becomes stale if the student takes a new assessment that significantly changes their proficiency profile. Stale paths prompt the system to offer a new path generation.

businessRules.title: Progress Calculation
businessRules.description: Progress = completed activities / total activities * 100. Skipped or removed activities do not count toward the total.

businessRules.title: Completion Trigger
businessRules.description: A path is marked complete when all activities are finished and the student's proficiency meets or exceeds the target level.

businessRules.title: Pause and Resume
businessRules.description: Paths can be paused by the teacher. Paused paths retain their state and can be resumed at any time.

businessRules.title: Level Sourcing
businessRules.description: Current Level and Target Level are derived from the most recent assessment data. Target Level is determined by grade-level benchmarks and teacher-adjustable goals.

businessRules.title: Activity Types
businessRules.description: Path activities can include practice assignments (Freckle), reading assignments (AR/myON), and focused skill exercises. The mix is determined by the adaptive engine.

states.name: Active
states.description: Path is generated and student is working through activities
states.triggers: System generates path after qualifying assessment

states.name: Complete
states.description: All activities finished and target level met
states.triggers: Last activity completed and proficiency target reached

states.name: Paused
states.description: Path is temporarily suspended by teacher
states.triggers: Teacher clicks pause; student activity is halted

states.name: Stale
states.description: New assessment data has significantly changed the student's profile
states.triggers: Student completes a new assessment with significantly different results

relationships.mechanics: Each Learning Path belongs to exactly one Student. The path adapts to the student's performance over time.
relationships.cardinality: One-to-one per subject. A Student has at most one active Path per subject; each Path belongs to one Student.
relationships.sorts: Paths for a student: by subject, by status.
relationships.dependencies: If a Student is deactivated, their paths are paused. If reactivated, paths can resume.

relationships.mechanics: Paths target specific skills for improvement. Focus skills determine the types of activities included in the sequence.
relationships.cardinality: Many-to-many. A Path targets multiple Skills; a Skill can be targeted by many Paths across students.
relationships.sorts: Skills within a path: by priority (most critical gap first).
relationships.filters: By skill domain, by proficiency level.
relationships.dependencies: If a Skill is retired, the path may need regeneration to focus on replacement skills.

relationships.mechanics: Paths contain an ordered sequence of assignments/activities. The system generates these automatically and teachers can modify the sequence.
relationships.cardinality: One-to-many. A Path contains multiple Activities (Assignments); each Activity belongs to one Path.
relationships.sorts: Activities within a path: by sequence order.
relationships.filters: By activity type, by completion status.
relationships.dependencies: If an Assignment is deleted externally, the path skips it and moves to the next activity.

relationships.mechanics: Paths are generated from assessment results. The assessment that triggered path generation is recorded for reference.
relationships.cardinality: Many-to-one (origin). A Path is generated from one Assessment; an Assessment can trigger multiple Paths (one per student).
relationships.dependencies: A new assessment may trigger path staleness and regeneration.

relationships.mechanics: Scores from completed path activities feed back into the adaptive engine to adjust upcoming activities.
relationships.cardinality: One-to-many. Path activities generate Scores; Scores inform path adaptation.
relationships.sorts: Scores from path activities: by date.
relationships.filters: By activity type, by proficiency level.
relationships.dependencies: Score availability is required for adaptive sequencing to function.

relationships.mechanics: Path activities may include resource recommendations (AR books, practice sets) alongside formal assignments.
relationships.cardinality: Many-to-many. A Path may reference multiple Resources; a Resource can appear in multiple Paths.
relationships.sorts: Resources within a path: by sequence position.
relationships.dependencies: If a Resource is removed from the catalog, the path substitutes an equivalent.

relationships.mechanics: Teachers monitor and customize student paths. They can accept/reject recommendations, pause paths, and view progress.
relationships.cardinality: Many-to-one. A Teacher's students have multiple Paths; each Path's student belongs to the Teacher's class(es).
relationships.sorts: Paths for a teacher's students: by student name, by progress, by status.
relationships.filters: By subject, by status, by progress range.
relationships.dependencies: If a teacher changes, the new teacher inherits visibility of student paths.

relationships.mechanics: Path data informs student grouping. Students with similar path profiles may be grouped together.
relationships.cardinality: Many-to-many (indirect). Path proficiency data can inform group creation.
relationships.dependencies: Group creation from path data is informational and does not modify paths.

nestedObjects.name: Assignment
nestedObjects.description: Ordered sequence of activities the student works through

nestedObjects.name: Resource
nestedObjects.description: Recommended practice resources included in the path

allAttributes.name: Path Name
allAttributes.description: Auto-generated display name based on subject and focus skills.

allAttributes.name: Student
allAttributes.description: The student this path belongs to.

allAttributes.name: Subject
allAttributes.description: Subject area: ela or math. Determines the activity types and skill targets.

allAttributes.name: Focus Skills
allAttributes.description: Skills targeted by this path, ordered by priority (most critical gap first).

allAttributes.name: Current Level
allAttributes.description: Student's current proficiency level in the path's subject. Format varies by subject (GE, Lexile, Scaled Score).

allAttributes.name: Target Level
allAttributes.description: Target proficiency level. Derived from grade-level benchmarks, adjustable by teacher.

allAttributes.name: Activities
allAttributes.description: Ordered sequence of activities composing this path.

allAttributes.name: Status
allAttributes.description: Current state: active, complete, paused, or stale.

allAttributes.name: Generated From
allAttributes.description: The assessment whose results triggered this path's generation.

allAttributes.name: Last Updated
allAttributes.description: When the path was last adapted based on new student data.

allAttributes.name: Estimated Remaining
allAttributes.description: Estimated time to complete remaining activities. Expressed in natural language (e.g., '2 weeks', '3 days').

structure.evidence: A Learning Path has rich attributes: name, student, subject, focus skills, current/target levels, activities, progress, status, generated from, last updated, and estimated remaining. You can design a detail page for a Path.

instances.evidence: 'Math Foundations — Number Sense' (Harry, 35% progress, active, 2 weeks remaining), 'Reading Comprehension Boost' (Hermione, 80% progress, 3 days remaining), 'Geometry Mastery' (Ron, 100% complete). Each student can have multiple Path instances across subjects.

purpose.evidence: Learning Paths personalize instruction by sequencing activities to address individual skill gaps. Students follow their path, teachers monitor and customize, and the adaptive engine adjusts the sequence based on ongoing performance data.

sipValidation.verdict: Domain activity object. Personalized adaptive learning sequence that connects assessment data to targeted instruction through ordered activities.

synonyms.notes: Shortened form used in most UI surfaces
Slightly more formal synonym; used interchangeably
Technical term emphasizing the adaptive algorithm

shapeshifterMatrix.description: A student's active learning path with progress, focus skills, and estimated time remaining. Students see where they are and start their next activity.
Learning paths for students in a class, listed with progress and status. Teachers monitor pacing and customize paths for individual students.
The complete learning path — skill targets, activity sequence, progress, and all management controls. Teachers review path logic, adjust levels, or regenerate the path entirely.
